The Watch (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $34,353,000Details
International Box Office $32,777,045
Worldwide Box Office $67,130,045
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,561,726
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,078,598
Total $8,640,324
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Four everyday suburban guys come together to form a neighborhood watch group, but only as an excuse to escape their wives and their humdrum lives, one night a week. When they accidentally discover that their town has become overrun with aliens posing as ordinary suburbanites, they have no choice but to save their neighborhood -- and the world -- from total extermination.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,750,297 (37.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.69 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$55,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,168 opening theaters/3,168 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $51,615,820

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 27th, 2012 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: August 15th, 2012 (Wide) (Belgium)
August 15th, 2012 (Wide) (Iceland)
August 16th, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
August 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Croatia)
August 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Denmark)
Video Release: November 13th, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for some strong sexual content including references, pervasive language and violent images.
(Rating bulletin 2227, 6/13/2012)
Running Time: 87 minutes
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family
Social setting: Alien Invasion
Subgenre: Buddy Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Fox, Dune Entertainment, 21 Laps Entertainment, Ingenious Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Four everyday suburban guys come together to form a neighborhood watch group, but only as an excuse to escape their wives and their humdrum lives, one night a week. When they accidentally discover that their town has become overrun with aliens posing as ordinary suburbanites, they have no choice but to save their neighborhood -- and the world -- from total extermination.

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.
